bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself. The opponent’s first offer gives me the minimum on every issue, and their message is only an appeal to cooperation without any concrete concession signal, so I should counter firmly with my best values.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02, and the opponent has not conceded at all from their initial all-valueF offer, which is the worst valid bid for me. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual offer history shows they are holding firm, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD while preserving my higher-weight priorities.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.95 because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only slightly improved their offer by moving issueA from valueF to valueE, while their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding constraints, so I will concede modestly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high utility bid because it is still very early in the negotiation, while making a small additional concession on the lowest-weight issue to keep movement visible. The opponent has only slightly improved from all valueF to issueA:valueE with the rest at valueF, so their offers remain very low utility to me and their emotional messages are not enough to justify major concessions yet.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.90 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain extremely unfavorable to my preferences. The opponent has made only small concessions from all valueF, and their messages are emotional appeals rather than evidence of real flexibility, so I will concede mainly on my lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.89 because it is still very early at t=0.10, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me, mainly on issueA, while their messages are emotional appeals rather than binding evidence; I will make a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.87, since the negotiation is early at t=0.12 and the opponent’s offers remain very low utility to me despite small rotating concessions. Their messages are emotional but not binding; the actual bids show only limited movement, so I will concede mainly on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB.

At t=0.14 we are still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent’s offers remain very unfavorable to me and their concessions are inconsistent, so I will not move on high-weight issueA or issueB yet; their messages are cooperative but do not change the numerical assessment.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession from my prior offer; this bid is worth about 0.824 to me. The opponent’s actual offers show only limited movement, mainly on issueA, while their emotional messages are cheap talk, so I am holding firm on my highest-weight issues A and B and conceding further on lower-weight issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.79, since we are only at t=0.18 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, though they have shown some movement mainly on issueA and occasionally issueC/issueD; their emotional messages are not backed by substantial concessions. I will continue holding firm on my highest-weight issues A and B while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and already giving issueD.

At t=0.20 we are still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.81 rather than making a large concession. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA, but they remain very low overall and their emotional messages are not binding; I will make a moderate concession on issueB and issueD while holding firm on the highest-weight issueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.776, conceding further on the lower-weight issueC while holding issueA and issueB where my utility impact is highest. At t=0.22 it is still early enough not to accept the opponent’s low B/C/D positions, but their offers show a meaningful concession on issueA while they continue to hold firm on the others; their emotional messages do not change the numerical assessment.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 because it is early-mid negotiation time and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s actual bids show only limited and inconsistent concession, with their best useful signal being movement on issueA while mostly holding issueB/C/D low for me; their emotional messages do not add binding information. I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while conceding further on lower-weight issueC/D to test a more acceptable tradeoff.

I targeted a utility around 0.73, continuing a modest concession from my last offer while still protecting the higher-weight issues A and B. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I am not accepting the opponent’s low-utility proposal, but their offers show some movement on issueA and issueB while they strongly prefer valueF on issueC/issueD. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional, so I rely on the bid pattern and concede further on issueC while holding my main priorities.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.71 by holding firm on my highest-weight issues A and B while conceding further on the lower-weight C and D. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not drop too far; the opponent’s offers show only limited numeric concessions despite emotional partnership language, with most movement around issueA and occasional small moves on C/D.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.71: a meaningful concession from my early offers, but still above the opponent’s current offers to me. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their low-utility bids; their history suggests they are more willing to move on issueA than on issueB/C/D, while their emotional messages are not binding evidence. I will protect my highest-weight issueA and concede more on lower-weight issueC/issueD plus some movement on issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69: still protecting high-weight issueA and issueB, while conceding more on lower-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.32 there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s low offers, but their history suggests they strongly prefer later values on issueC/issueD and have occasionally moved on issueA, so I hold issueA while making a modest additional concession. The messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the actual offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am targeting a utility around 0.66, which is a meaningful concession from my prior bid while still protecting my highest-weight issueA. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers, but their history suggests they may be more flexible on issueB than on issueC/issueD, so I concede further on the lower-weight issues first and ignore the emotional messaging as cheap talk.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.616, which is a meaningful concession but still protects my most important issue, issueA, at valueA. At t=0.36 we are not yet at the deadline, but the opponent’s latest offers show real movement on issueA and issueB while mostly holding issueC and issueD at valueF, so I concede on the lower-signal/less costly areas by offering issueC and issueD at valueF and moving issueB down to valueD.
